# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

image: python:latest

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deploy

variables:
  # Set PIP_CACHE_DIR to a local path within the project to make it cacheable by GitLab CI
  PIP_CACHE_DIR: ".cache/pip"

# Define a cache for pip dependencies to speed up consecutive pipeline runs
cache:
  paths:
    - .cache/pip # Cache the directory where pip stores downloaded packages
  key: "$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG" # Use a unique key per branch/tag

# A hidden job (template) for installing Python dependencies
# This ensures that all jobs needing dependencies share the same installation logic
.install_python_dependencies:
  before_script:
    - python -m pip install --upgrade pip
    # Install dependencies from requirements.txt
    # This command by default fetches the latest compatible versions
    # and does not perform hash verification unless explicitly configured.
    # To achieve "latest packages automatically" and "without pinned versions",
    # ensure your requirements.txt lists packages without strict version pins
    # (e.g., "package_name" or "package_name>=1.0" instead of "package_name==1.2.3").
    - pip install -r requirements.txt

test_job:
  stage: test
  extends: .install_python_dependencies
  script:
    - echo "Running tests..."
    # Install pytest if not already included in requirements.txt
    - pip install pytest
    - pytest # Assumes tests are configured to be found and run by pytest

build_job:
  stage: build
  extends: .install_python_dependencies
  script:
    - echo "Building package..."
    # Install tools required for building Python distribution packages
    - pip install build twine
    # Build source distribution (.tar.gz) and wheel distribution (.whl)
    - python -m build --sdist --wheel .
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/ # Archive the 'dist/' directory containing the built packages
    expire_in: 1 week # How long to keep the artifacts
    name: "$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA-build"

deploy_job:
  stage: deploy
  # This job will run only on the default branch (e.g., 'main' or 'master').
  # Adjust the 'only' rule as needed for your deployment strategy
  # (e.g., specific tags, manual triggers).
  only:
    - main
    - master
  script:
    - echo "Deploying application..."
    - echo "Artifacts from the 'build' stage (located in 'dist/') are available here for deployment."
    # --- Replace this section with your actual deployment logic ---
    # Examples:
    # 1. Deploying a Python package to PyPI (e.g., using 'twine'):
    #    - twine upload --repository-url $PYPI_URL --username $PYPI_USERNAME --password $PYPI_PASSWORD dist/*
    # 2. Building and pushing a Docker image to a container registry:
    #    - docker login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" -p "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" "$CI_REGISTRY"
    #    - docker build -t "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA" .
    #    - docker push "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA"
    # 3. Deploying to a cloud provider (e.g., AWS S3, Google Cloud Run, Azure App Service):
    #    - aws s3 cp dist/my_app.zip s3://my-app-bucket/
    #    - gcloud run deploy my-service --image gcr.io/my-project/my-image --platform managed --region us-central1
    # Ensure your GitLab CI runner has the necessary credentials and tools configured for your chosen deployment method.
    - echo "Deployment placeholder completed. Please customize 'deploy_job' script."
